title: Elaphoglossum lastii
intro: Elaphoglossum lastii is a species of fern, belonging to the family Dryopteridaceae, native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including inflammation, fever, and respiratory issues, due to its potential antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Elaphoglossum lastii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Elaphoglossum lastii is commonly prepared as a decoction or infusion, and is also used in the production of traditional teas and tinctures.
taxonomy: Elaphoglossum lastii is a species within the genus Elaphoglossum, which belongs to the family Dryopteridaceae. This classification places it among the true ferns, a group of vascular plants characterized by their unique reproductive structures. The family Dryopteridaceae is part of the larger group known as the leptosporangiate ferns.
